title: The Turing Transformation
author: Agrawal, Gans & Goldfarb
desc: Task automation enhances jobs and reduces inequality by lowering the skill premium — opening tasks locked behind rare skills to a wider pool.

title: Antiqua et Nova
author: Vatican / Pope Leo XIV
desc: AI is a tool, not a species. Emphatically augmentation: AI should enhance human dignity, not replace human agency.

title: Labor Market Impacts of AI
author: Autor
desc: AI could restore middle-skill jobs by enabling less-trained workers to do expert work. Creates new tasks, net positive for labor demand — if policy steers it.

title: AI as Normal Technology
author: Narayanan & Kapoor
desc: AI is a GPT like electricity. Meaningful productivity impact but slow diffusion — decades, not years. No superintelligence. Existing institutions can adapt.

title: Techno-Optimist Manifesto
author: Andreessen
desc: Technology makes everyone more productive, anyone can build. Gains diffuse naturally through competition. Pure faith.

title: Impact of AI on Labour Market
author: Tony Blair Institute
desc: AI saves ~25% of UK workforce time. 1-3M jobs displaced but offset by new demand. GDP +11% by 2050.

title: d/acc: My Techno-Optimism
author: Vitalik Buterin
desc: Defensive, decentralized, differential acceleration. Cautious on AI power but strongly prescribes building tech that diffuses power.

title: The Turing Trap
author: Brynjolfsson
desc: The choice between augmentation and replacement IS the choice between diffuse and concentrated gains.

title: Simple Macroeconomics of AI
author: Acemoglu
desc: Skeptical of large GDP gains. Only modest share of tasks automatable. But what gains exist concentrate with capital owners without active policy.

title: The Reverse Centaur's Guide
author: Cory Doctorow
desc: AI is a bubble and a worker-frightening machine. Can't do your job, but the narrative breaks labor power.

title: Will A.I. Become the New McKinsey?
author: Ted Chiang
desc: AI is technically overhyped. Its real function is accountability laundering — companies fire workers even when AI doesn't work.

title: Work Transformed
author: ILO
desc: 1 in 4 workers globally exposed. Some augmentation potential but adoption concentrated in rich countries. Labour income share falling.

title: Gradual Disempowerment
author: Kulveit, Douglas et al.
desc: Incremental AI erodes human economic participation. Institutions slowly misalign from human interests. No sudden break — a structural drift.

title: Keep the Future Human
author: Aguirre
desc: Close the gates to AGI, build Tool AI instead. Warns unchecked AGI race leads to replacement + concentrated power.

title: The Intelligence Curse
author: Drago & Laine
desc: AI creates rentier-state dynamics: non-human factors dominate, powerful actors lose incentive to invest in people.

title: Something Big Is Happening
author: Matt Shumer
desc: This is February 2020 for AI. CEO says he's no longer needed for technical work. 85M views. Personal testimony.

title: Machines of Loving Grace
author: Amodei
desc: Country of geniuses in a datacenter. Strong replacement-level capability. Hopes for diffusion but depends on governance.

title: Moore's Law for Everything
author: Altman
desc: AI replaces most labor, costs tend to zero. Default is concentrated; proposes American Equity Fund.

title: The 2028 Intelligence Crisis
author: Citrini Research
desc: Fictional 2028: Ghost GDP, S&P down 38%, mass white-collar unemployment. Triggered 800-point Dow drop.

title: Situational Awareness
author: Aschenbrenner
desc: AGI by ~2027, superintelligence by decade's end. Trillion-dollar clusters. Government Project takes over.

title: AI 2027
author: Kokotajlo, Alexander et al.
desc: Superhuman AI by ~2027, intelligence explosion, one lab dominates. Most extreme replacement + concentration scenario.

title: Promethean Fire
author: Toloui
desc: Fast-follower + labor-augmenting. Decentralized AI enhances human capability. Competition keeps prices low.

title: Copilot Empire
author: Toloui
desc: Winner-take-all + labor-augmenting. Few dominant platforms own the AI layer but humans remain essential.

title: Disruption Swarm
author: Toloui
desc: Fast-follower + labor-replacing. Rapid diffusion, commoditization. Massive displacement but no single actor captures gains.

title: Titan's Dominion
author: Toloui
desc: Winner-take-all + labor-replacing. Monopolistic control of broadly displacing technology. The worst quadrant.

title: AI Augments Workers
author: Korinek / IMF
desc: AI makes workers more productive without replacing them. Standard productivity boost. Gains spread through labor markets.

title: Full AGI Scenario
author: Korinek / IMF
desc: AGI replaces most cognitive labor. Capital owners benefit enormously. Without redistribution, extreme inequality.

title: Plateau
author: CFG
desc: AI hits technical limits. Smaller, cheaper, local models. Open-source thrives. A bright winter.

title: Centralized Takeoff
author: CFG
desc: Fast capability growth controlled by one dominant actor. Unprecedented power concentration. Maximum misalignment risk.

title: Decentralized Takeoff
author: CFG
desc: Fast capability growth across many actors. Open-weight models enable innovation but create misuse risks.

title: Steady Progress
author: DSIT
desc: AI improves steadily, adoption gradual. Existing institutions adapt. Most conservative official scenario.

title: Rapid Transformation
author: DSIT
desc: Fast capability gains, significant displacement. Few dominant companies lead. Government struggles to keep pace.
